messages.content:
Which of the following types of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions fall under the category of Scope 3 emissions?


GHG emissions that companies generate in their value chain, both upstream and downstream

GHG emissions that companies generate from their owned and controlled resources

GHG emissions that companies offset from their owned and controlled resources

GHG emissions that companies generate from purchased energy
GHG emissions that companies generate in their value chain, both upstream and downstream.

What is the “City Sprint” process?


A series of workshops involving business and public service leaders in which city stakeholders can evaluate and prioritize net-zero solutions for near-term implementation in their city.

A solution that helps build an ecosystem where participants can share data and promote water reuse within and between industries.

A way to decarbonize, generate new jobs in underserved areas, and deliver vital benefits like better air quality and health.

A method to analyze Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions across multiple industries by considering target-setting approaches and measurement standards and methodologies.
A series of workshops involving business and public service leaders in which city stakeholders can evaluate and prioritize net-zero solutions for near-term implementation in their city.

Which of the following priority areas for technology and transformation can accelerate the delivery of modernized and enhanced electricity, gas, and water infrastructures?


Connected lower-carbon infrastructure (CLCI)

Grid technology

Clean power

Electric mobility (e-mobility)
Connected lower-carbon infrastructure (CLCI)

Which of the following is not the responsibility of the chief financial officer (CFO) in an organization’s sustainability measurement activities?


Aligning non-financial aspects of performance with the financial corporate strategy

Building innovative business cases to demonstrate the value of sustainability

Measuring only the financial growth of the organization
Measuring only the financial growth of the organization.

Under the Non-Financial Reporting Directive (NFRD) from the European Union (EU), what are large public-interest companies required to disclose?


Reveal how they operate and address social and environmental challenges.

Reveal the financial data of their operations in Europe.

Disclose the steps they take to upskill their employees on ethics and compliance issues.
Reveal how they operate and address social and environmental challenges.

What does the “economy of care” focus on?


The increasing rate of population growth and the subsequent need for greater resources

The importance of qualitative key performance indicators (KPIs)

The business strategies adopted by various companies to upskill employees

The importance of qualitative key performance indicators (KPIs) for measuring sustainability, such as environmental and social impacts, and shifting from economic growth to well-being and care for people and the planet.

Which of the following is a European Union (EU)-wide classification system for sustainable activities?


EU Taxonomy

Green New Deal

Paris Agreement
EU Taxonomy.

Which of the following is not a consequence for businesses that fail to integrate sustainability measurement in their operations?


They could be penalized in terms of poor environmental, social, and governance (ESG) goals.

They may lose out on critical growth opportunities.

They will be able to respond to market changes in a more agile manner.
They will be able to respond to market changes in a more agile manner.

Budget updates, revising planning for specific business units, and establishing incentives for achieving sustainability goals are examples of actions for which key area of sustainability performance measurement?


Analyze

Adapt

Identify

Aggregate
Adapt.

Which measuring technique is useful in benchmarking a company’s environmental, social, and governance (ESG) reporting with the leading frameworks on sustainability and ESG disclosures and reporting?


Sustainability Gap analysis

Materiality score

Crisis management communication plan

Sustainability roadmap
Sustainability Gap analysis.

How can artificial intelligence (AI), big data, machine learning (ML), and data analytics facilitate better and faster business decisions?


By enabling better coverage and integration of financial data

By providing companies with mature data and enhanced reporting capabilities

By filtering out non-verifiable data that can disrupt the decision-making process

By providing companies with mature data and enhanced reporting capabilities, allowing for improved forecasting, risk assessment, and strategic planning. AI, big data, ML, and data analytics can help companies identify patterns and trends in data that may not be visible through traditional data analysis methods. This can lead to faster and more accurate decision-making, as well as better-informed strategies.

_______ is Accenture’s custom-built, comprehensive, and transparent cloud platform that offers quick access to reliable and environmental, social, and governance (ESG)-pertinent data lake solutions.


CFO ESG Maturity Matrix

ESG 360

Sustainability performance measurement (SPM) solution
ESG 360.

Intelligent solutions through digital transformation drive results from every aspect of the responsible value chain. Which of the following is an example of how intelligent solutions drive impact at the design stage?


Digitizing the research and development processes

Introducing product platform engineering and modernization

Leveraging the digital twin technology to help clients optimize design to build products with low carbon footprint
Leveraging the digital twin technology to help clients optimize design to build products with low carbon footprint.

Which of the following are the three most important areas that comprise a responsible value chain?


Trust, Use Extension, Sustainable

Net Zero, Circular, Use Extension

Trust, Net Zero, Circular

Trust, Net Zero, Leadership
Trust, Net Zero, Circular

Aercrux Corporation Pvt. Ltd. has decided to embrace the opportunities of a responsible value chain along the three pillars of Trust, Net Zero, and Circular. Which of the following actions can the company take toward achieving Net Zero?


Transition to 100% renewable electricity in production and become carbon negative for all their direct and indirect emissions.

Provide a living wage or income to everyone who directly provides goods and services to the company.

Make their materials packaging returnable or recyclable.
Transition to 100% renewable electricity in production and become carbon negative for all their direct and indirect emissions.

Identify the key drivers that enable businesses to win stakeholder trust in a responsible value chain.


Ethical sourcing of raw materials and supply chain traceability

Responsible treatment of hazardous substances and supply chain traceability

Fair wages and health and safety for all workers

Supply chain traceability, supplier ethics and transparency, working conditions including fair wages and health and safety, and zero corruption
Supply chain traceability, supplier ethics and transparency, working conditions including fair wages and health and safety, and zero corruption.

What is the aim of the European Union’s Sustainable Financial Disclosure Regulation?


It is aimed at obtaining transparent, traceable, and quality environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data from companies across their value chains.

It is aimed at ensuring transparency in the sourcing of raw materials and managing relationships in the value chain.

It is aimed at ensuring asset managers, financial advisors, and other financial market participants consider sustainability and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in their decision-making around investments.

It is aimed at ensuring that a company has a strong environmental agenda and becomes an environmentally responsible organization.

It is aimed at ensuring asset managers, financial advisors, and other financial market participants consider sustainability and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in their decision-making around investments.

Identify the five important elements that businesses need to examine in any ambition-setting exercise.


Capabilities; processes; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; operating models; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; operating models; systems; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives
Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; operating models; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives.

A leading consumer goods firm has adopted Accenture’s 360° Value framework. A responsible value chain is a significant component of this framework. How can this contribute to the consumer experience and growth of their brand?


A responsible value chain will help the company to provide complete transparency to their consumers on the ecological and ethical footprint of the company’s products and services.

A responsible value chain will help the company to establish responsible production and consumption practices throughout its manufacturing processes.

A responsible value chain will enable the company to reduce its carbon footprint and improve its social responsibility measures.

A responsible value chain will help the company to provide complete transparency to their consumers on the ecological and ethical footprint of the company’s products and services, which can help to build customer trust and loyalty. By showing a commitment to responsible practices throughout their value chain, the company can differentiate itself from competitors and strengthen its brand reputation. This can lead to increased customer satisfaction and ultimately drive growth for the brand.
